zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Salmon於大約1495年出生在英國時，她的父親John Salmon22歲，母親Mrs. John Salmon22歲。她於1508年與John Pysing在英國多瑟特阿波次波利結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1545年享年51歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Salmon于大约1495年出生在英国时，她的父亲John Salmon22岁，母亲Mrs. John Salmon22岁。她于1508年与John Pysing在英国多瑟特阿波次波利结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1545年享年51岁，埋葬在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Salmon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and French: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Salmon, Saumon, a shortened form of Salomon (see Solomon ). The Normans introduced it to England, and it was current as a personal name there until at least the end of the 14th century. The derived surname is found throughout 13th- and 14th-century England, mostly in the spellings Saleman and Salman, which eventually became regularized as Salmon. See also 2 below, compare Salman 3.

English: occasionally perhaps a nickname for someone ‘as wise as Solomon’. In Chaucer's Canon's Yeoman's Tale, it is stated: ‘And whan we been togidres euerichoon, Euery man semeth a Salomon’. King Solomon was famed for his wisdom.
English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French, Middle English salmon, saumo(u)n, saumun, samoun ‘salmon’, no doubt given to someone who caught, sold, or ate the fish.

variants.name: Sammon
meanings: English and Irish (Mayo): variant of   Salmon  .

variants.name: Salmen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Wilhelm, Ilse.
English (Middlesex) and German: variant of English   Salmon   or Swiss German   Salman  .

variants.name: Salmons
meanings: English: variant of Salmon , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Salmond
meanings: Scottish (Angus and Fife) and English: variant of Salmon 1, with excrescent -d.

variants.name: Salman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Mohammad, Salman, Faisal, Ahmad, Amira, Imad, Issam, Khalid, Mohamed, Mohammed, Moosa, Omar.
Muslim: from a personal name based on Arabic salmān ‘safe’.

variants.name: Solomon
meanings: Jewish, English, Scottish, Dutch, Swedish, French, Portuguese, Romanian, Ethiopian, and Eritrean; Spanish (Solomón): from a vernacular form of the Biblical Hebrew personal name Shelomo, a derivative of shalom ‘peace’. The name Solomon has for generations been a popular Jewish name and was also fairly widespread in the Middle Ages among Christians. In the Bible it is the name of King David's successor, noted for his wisdom; among Christians it was therefore also used as a nickname for a man who was considered wise. The spelling Solomon is used in the King James Bible of 1611, which is why this is the standard form of the name in modern English, but spellings with Sal- were more usual across continental Europe, the 1534 Lutheran Bible rendering it as Salomo and the 1560 Geneva Bible as Salomon . From the 7th century the spelling Salomon is recorded as a Christian personal name in France, where it was the name of several saints including a Breton king martyred in 874. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages (see Salomon , Salamon ). See also Salmon 1. — Note: Since Ethiopians and Eritreans do not have hereditary surnames, the Ethiopian and Eritrean name was registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

variants.name: Salkin
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Salkin, probably a pet form (with the Flemish and Middle English diminutive suffix -kin) of Salomon (see Salmon and Solomon ).
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish personal name Zalkind, derived from Salomon .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Salmon was born about 1495, in England, her father, John Salmon, was 22 and her mother, Mrs. John Salmon, was 22. She married John Pysing in 1508, in Abbotsbury, Dorset, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1545, at the age of 51, and was buried in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Salmon est née environ 1495, à Angleterre, son père, John Salmon, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Mrs. John Salmon, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé John Pysing en 1508, à Abbotsbury, Dorset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1545, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterrée à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Salmon um 1495 in England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Salmon 22 und ihre Mutter Mrs. John Salmon 22. Sie hat 1508 in Abbotsbury, Dorset, England John Pysing geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1545 im Alter von 51 Jahren und wurde in England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Salmon è nata il circa 1495 nel England, sua padre, John Salmon, aveva 22 anni e sua madre, Mrs. John Salmon, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato John Pysing nel 1508, nel Abbotsbury, Dorset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1545, all'età di 51 anni, e fu sepolta a Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Salmonが約1495年にイギリスで生まれた時，父John Salmonは22歳，母Mrs. John Salmonは22歳であった。1508年にイギリスドールセトアボトスブリーでJohn Pysingと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1545年に51歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Salmon(이)가 대략 1495년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Salmon(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. John Salmon(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1508년 영국 도르세트 애벗스버이에서 John Pysing(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1545년 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Salmon nasceu aproximadamente 1495, em Inglaterra, seu pai, John Salmon, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. John Salmon, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com John Pysing em 1508, em Abbotsbury, Dorset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1545, com 51 anos, e foi sepultada em Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Salmon родилась около 1495 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени John Salmon было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Mrs. John Salmon, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Pysing в 1508 г. в местности эббоцбури, дорсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1545 г. в возрасте 51 год и была похоронена в местности Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Salmon nació aproximadamente 1495, en Inglaterra, su papá, John Salmon, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Mrs. John Salmon, tenía 22 años. Se casó con John Pysing en 1508, en Abbotsbury, Dorset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1545, a la edad de 51 años, y fue sepultada en Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
